message.content: Adversaries may create new cloud instances or virtual machines (VMs) within a cloud account to evade defenses and carry out malicious activities. This technique allows them to bypass existing firewall rules and permissions on current instances. By creating snapshots of volumes, mounting them on new instances, and applying less restrictive security policies, adversaries can collect data from local systems or stage data remotely. This method enables malicious activities without disrupting the execution of existing instances. Notably, the LAPSUS$ group has exploited this technique by creating new VMs in compromised cloud environments after gaining credential access. Mitigation strategies include implementing strict access controls, monitoring for unusual instance creation activities, and employing automated tools to detect and respond to unauthorized cloud resource modifications.
